attributes.action: Scrap interest on all student loan repayments
attributes.additional_details: Student loan interest means graduates often repay far more than they originally borrowed. With interest charged at up to 3% above inflation (currently around 7% total), a typical 3-year degree costing ~£27,000 can grow to over £40,000 in real debt before repayment even begins. Many people remain in debt for 30 years despite regular payments. Removing interest would make repayments fairer and reduce long-term financial strain.
attributes.background: We are calling on the government to remove interest from student loan repayments. Graduates are charged interest of up to 3% above inflation, meaning they repay far more than borrowed. This creates a ‘graduate tax’, leaving many in debt for 30 yrs & making this far more expensive than original cost.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petition, which calls for similar action:

Abolish interest charges on student loans
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/756832
